snp2.2:1.1:
Tissa:
“Those peacefully eating millet, Job’s tears, green gram,
snp2.2:1.3: obtained in line with the Dhamma,
snp2.2:1.4: don’t desire sensual-pleasures or tell falsehoods.
snp2.2:2.1: But when eating what is well-made, well-prepared,
snp2.2:2.4: Kassapa, one consumes a raw stench.
snp2.2:3.1: Yet you, kinsman of Brahmā, say,
snp2.2:3.2: ‘Raw stench is not proper for me,’
snp2.2:3.4: and the well-prepared fleshes of birds.
snp2.2:3.5: So I ask you, Kassapa, the meaning of that:
snp2.2:3.6:
Of what sort is ‘raw stench’ for you?”
The Buddha Kassapa:
snp2.2:4.3: useless recitations, associating with the wives of others:
snp2.2:4.4: This is a raw stench, not the eating of meat.
snp2.2:5.1: Those people here who are unrestrained in sensuality,
snp2.2:5.2: greedy for flavors, mixed together with what’s impure,
snp2.2:5.4: This is a raw stench, not the eating of meat.
snp2.2:6.1: Those who are rough, pitiless, eating the flesh off your back,
snp2.2:6.3: habitually ungenerous, giving to no one:
snp2.2:6.4: This is a raw stench, not the eating of meat.
snp2.2:7.3: boasting, conceit & pride, befriending those of no integrity:
snp2.2:7.4: This is a raw stench, not the eating of meat.
snp2.2:8.1: Those of evil habits, debt-repudiators, informers,
snp2.2:8.3: vile men who do evil things:
snp2.2:8.4: This is a raw stench, not the eating of meat.
snp2.2:9.1: Those people here who are unrestrained toward beings,
snp2.2:9.2: taking what’s others’, intent on injury,
snp2.2:9.4: This is a raw stench, not the eating of meat.
snp2.2:10.1: Those who are very greedy, constantly intent on hindering and killing;
snp2.2:10.2: beings who, after passing away, go to darkness,
snp2.2:10.4: This is a raw stench, not the eating of meat.
snp2.2:11.1: No fish & meat, no fasting, no nakedness,
snp2.2:11.2: no shaven head, no tangled hair,
snp2.2:11.3: no rough animal skins, no performance of fire oblations,
snp2.2:11.4: or the many austerities to become an immortal in the world,
snp2.2:11.5: no chants, no oblations, no performance of sacrifices at the proper season—
snp2.2:11.6: purify a mortal who hasn’t crossed over doubt.
snp2.2:12.1: One should go about guarded with regard to those things, one’s faculties understood,
snp2.2:12.2: standing firm in the Dhamma, delighting in being straightforward & mild.
snp2.2:12.4: the enlightened one isn’t smeared by what’s heard or seen.”
snp2.2:13.1: Thus the Blessed One, explained the meaning again & again.
snp2.2:13.2: The one who had mastered chants understood it.
snp2.2:13.4: free from raw stench, unfettered, indomitable— proclaimed it.
snp2.2:14.1: Hearing the Awakened One’s well-spoken word—
snp2.2:14.2: free from raw stench, dispelling all stress—
snp2.2:14.3: the one with lowered mind paid homage to the Tathāgata,
snp2.2:14.4: chose the Going Forth right there.
